title: ‘It’s a special feeling’: Mickey Gasper never gave up on his Major League dream, and now he’s reaping the rewards
author: Trevor Hass
bullets: Mickey Gasper is batting .333 for the Red Sox in the 2026 MLB season after six years in the Minors.
Gasper made his Major League debut with the Red Sox in August 2024 but went hitless in 18 at-bats.
Red Sox interim manager Chad Tracy praises Gasper for his swing decisions and ability to play multiple positions.

title: Mass. family, Worcester woman identified as victims in deadly Virginia bus crash
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: A Greenfield couple, their two children, and a Worcester woman were killed in a Virginia bus crash.
The crash on I-81 involved a bus failing to slow down, causing a fatal multi-vehicle collision.
Bus driver Jing S. Dong faces charges, including involuntary manslaughter, after the incident.

title: Connor Wong’s 3-RBI night helped power Red Sox win, and it couldn’t come at a better time for him individually
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Connor Wong drove in three runs, aiding the Red Sox to victory over the Cleveland Guardians.
Wong's role diminished with emergence of other catchers like Mickey Gasper, now the Red Sox primary catcher.
Wong's batting average improved to .245 this season, making him a contender for secondary catcher role.

title: Sonny Gray continues to be great addition to Red Sox rotation, earns 6th win vs. Guardians
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Sonny Gray pitched six innings, allowing one run and striking out seven, in Red Sox's 9-1 win against the Guardians.
Gray has a 2.00 ERA over five starts in May, helping fill the gap left by Garrett Crochet's shoulder injury.
The 36-year-old is earning $31 million this year with a mutual option for 2027, amid a challenging season for Boston.

title: Here’s the full lineup of athletes and celebrities involved in Drake Maye’s charity softball game
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Drake Maye's charity softball game takes place at Polar Park in Worcester on Sunday afternoon.
Notable participants include Julian Edelman, Zdeno Chara, comedians Matt Rife and Dave Portnoy.
Proceeds from the game will benefit the MayeDay foundation, supporting children and families in need.

title: Potential A.J. Brown Patriots-Eagles trade reportedly has ‘framework in place’
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Patriots reportedly have a framework for acquiring A.J. Brown from Eagles, expected to finalize post-June 1.
Philadelphia faces reduced cap hit if Brown is traded after June 1; potential $43.5 million to $16 million hit.
Patriots and Eagles reportedly disagree on trade terms, with Patriots hesitant to trade a first-round draft pick.

title: Did you hear it? Here’s what we know about a big boom heard around Mass.
author: Heather Alterisio
bullets: Residents of Greater Boston reported hearing a loud boom and feeling tremors around 2:10 PM on Saturday.
Meteorologists identified a bolide meteor as the likely cause of the sonic boom, per satellite data.
No earthquakes or explosions were confirmed by USGS or emergency responders in connection with the boom.

title: Brayan Bello excels in bulk relief, but opener melts in Red Sox’ 4-3 loss to Guardians
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Opener Tyler Samaniego allowed four runs in the first inning, leading to a 4-3 Red Sox loss to the Guardians.
Brayan Bello excelled in relief, pitching seven scoreless innings with five strikeouts and no walks for the Red Sox.
The Red Sox have lost six of their last seven games, dropping to 23-33 and last place in the AL East.

title: Giannis Antetokounmpo praises TD Garden environment amid trade rumors
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Giannis Antetokounmpo praised TD Garden and Celtics fans during a recent livestream amid ongoing trade rumors.
Both the Boston Celtics and Miami Heat are considered top contenders to acquire Antetokounmpo this offseason.
Speculations involve whether Jaylen Brown could be part of a trade package for acquiring the Bucks superstar.

title: Harvard ‘Senior Sunrise’ cut short by cops
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Police cleared the Harvard Senior Sunrise event on Weeks Footbridge due to crowd concerns early Friday morning.
Massachusetts State Police, along with Cambridge and Harvard University Police, responded to reports of swimming attempts in the Charles River.
The gathering, part of a tradition for the Class of 2026, followed the previous day's commencement with Conan O'Brien as speaker.
